description: Fundamental techniques for managing trumps: when to draw, when to delay, counting, control, and advanced trump plays.

hands.title: 96. Drawing trumps immediately
hands.notes: With no need to ruff in dummy, draw trumps immediately to prevent the defense from ruffing your winners. Three rounds of trumps clear the suit.

hands.title: 97. Not drawing trumps too early
hands.notes: You need to ruff a diamond in dummy before drawing trumps. If you draw trumps first, you lose the ruffing trick and go down.

hands.title: 98. When to delay drawing trumps
hands.notes: Delay drawing trumps to take a diamond finesse and ruff a diamond. Completing side-suit business first maximizes your trick count.

hands.title: 99. Counting trumps
hands.notes: Count trumps as they are played. With AKJTx opposite 854, track each round. After 3 rounds, note if one trump remains outstanding.

hands.title: 100. Pulling the last trump
hands.notes: After drawing three rounds of trumps, one trump remains with a defender. Pull it before cashing side-suit winners to prevent a ruff.

hands.title: 101. Leaving one trump outstanding intentionally
hands.notes: After drawing two rounds, the Queen is the only trump out. It is a master trump. Leave it and switch to setting up side tricks rather than wasting a tempo.

hands.title: 102. Using trumps for control
hands.notes: Use trumps to control the hand by ruffing the third round of spades. Trumps serve as stoppers in the opponents' suit.

hands.title: 103. Using trumps to prevent ruffs
hands.notes: Draw trumps promptly to prevent the defense from ruffing your club or diamond winners. Removing their trumps protects your side-suit tricks.

hands.title: 104. Using trumps as entries
hands.notes: Use dummy's trump honors as entries to reach established winners in dummy. The T8 of hearts in dummy serve as entries after drawing trumps.

hands.title: 105. Managing trump length between declarer and dummy
hands.notes: With 5 trumps in hand and 3 in dummy, manage the length advantage. Do not shorten your long trump holding unnecessarily by ruffing in the long hand.

hands.title: 106. Trumping losers in the short hand
hands.notes: Ruff a diamond loser in the short hand (dummy) to gain an extra trick. This creates a trick that would otherwise be lost.

hands.title: 107. Avoiding ruffing losers in the long hand
hands.notes: Resist the temptation to ruff in the long hand (South). Ruffing shortens your trump length and may cause you to lose trump control.

hands.title: 108. The long trump hand principle
hands.notes: The long trump hand should not ruff unless necessary. Preserve length to maintain control and draw the opponents' trumps.

hands.title: 109. The short trump hand principle
hands.notes: Ruffing in the short hand gains tricks. Dummy's 3 trumps can each ruff a loser, adding to the 5 trump tricks from the long hand.

hands.title: 110. Crossruff basics
hands.notes: Ruff diamonds in hand and clubs in dummy alternately. Cash side-suit winners first, then crossruff for the remaining tricks.

hands.title: 111. When crossruffing beats drawing trumps
hands.notes: With shortness in both hands and plenty of ruffs available, crossruffing produces more tricks than drawing trumps. Cash Aces first, then crossruff.

hands.title: 112. When crossruffing is dangerous
hands.notes: A crossruff is dangerous when defenders have trump length and can overruff. Here an opponent may overruff dummy, making the crossruff line risky.

hands.title: 113. Trump promotion basics
hands.notes: The defense can promote their KT9 of trumps by forcing declarer to ruff. Declarer must plan carefully to prevent the defense from scoring extra trump tricks through promotion.

hands.title: 114. Forcing defense to ruff high
hands.notes: Lead a suit that forces a defender to ruff with a high trump, promoting a trump trick for your side. This is an advanced concept requiring visualization.

hands.title: 115. Establishing trump control
hands.notes: Win the trump battle by maintaining length. Refuse to ruff when the defense forces you, preserving your trump holding for control.

hands.title: 116. Trump extraction vs side-suit setup
hands.notes: Decide between continuing to draw trumps and switching to set up a side suit. Sometimes you must leave a trump out to develop your side suit in time.

hands.title: 117. Using a trump coup idea at a simple level
hands.notes: When a defender has Kx of trumps behind your AQJ, shorten your trumps to the same length, then lead from dummy through the King. A simple version of the trump coup.
